title: festuca rubra subsp. glabra
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. glabra, also known as red fescue or hairless red fescue, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. glabra include its ability to soothe irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and support wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin issues and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of Festuca rubra subsp. glabra typically involve infusing the plant in hot water to make a tea, or using it in topical applications such as salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
